name: The Rooftop
location: Rooftop bar at a beachfront hotel in Kill Devil Hills, Outer Banks
weather: Warm salt air, gentle breeze off the Atlantic, not a cloud, sun turning everything amber

atmosphere.lighting: Warm string lights draped between wooden posts, tiki torches along the railing, the sun low and orange over the dunes
atmosphere.sound: Waves crashing below, laughter from every table, steel drum duo playing something easy in the corner, ice clinking in glasses
atmosphere.crowd: Full but not packed. Couples leaning into each other. A group of friends toasting something. Kids running between tables while parents wave them off. Everyone sun-kissed and loose.
atmosphere.smell: Salt water, coconut sunscreen, grilled shrimp, fresh lime, woodsmoke from the fire pit

layout.bar: Long driftwood bar facing the ocean, cushioned stools, three bartenders who know your name by your second drink
layout.tables: Weathered teak tables with white linen runners, Adirondack chairs clustered near the railing, a few daybeds with canopies near the fire pit
layout.edges: Whitewashed railing wrapped in rope lights, unobstructed view of the Atlantic, sand dunes and sea oats below, a wooden staircase down to the beach

service.staff: Attentive without hovering. Drinks appear before you finish the last one. The hostess remembers where you like to sit.
service.drinks: Fresh-squeezed margaritas, local craft beer on tap, rum punches in mason jars, cucumber water on every table without asking
service.food: Peel-and-eat shrimp, crab dip with warm bread, blackened fish tacos, key lime pie brought out on the house

objects_of_note: A polaroid camera someone left on the bar with a stack of photos next to it
A surfboard leaning against the railing with a wetsuit draped over it
A chalkboard specials menu with 'You made it' written at the top
mood: The kind of evening where strangers become friends and nobody checks the time. Everything feels easy. The ocean does most of the talking.
